summary: UR189 is a CADASTRAL LAND-PARCEL REGISTER dated 23 NOVEMBER 1487 CE via Mode C (C1=12 flag, C3=49 year-offset -> 1438+49=1487, C5=11 month, C6=23 day) -- HIGH-CONFIDENCE DATE, validated by independent ALBA session. Second member of the QUAI BRANLY TRIO (UR188 + UR189 + UR192). 88 cords, 0-SEUL architecture with HEAVY L2 SUBSIDIARY TREES (L2/L1=0.80), 6 clusters, 6 colors AB(39 44%) + W(21 24%) + MB(19 22%) + BG(7 8%) + KB(1) + GG(1). 5 STRING at 100 percent dict hit: maqa x3 (BOUNDARY MARKER, crosses 3 colors MB->AB->W), pata x1 (TERRACE / PLOT, MB -- closing word confirms register IS about land), pika x1 (WHO-PASSIVE = 'who was assigned / affected?' -- responsibility query on MB L2 of C27). TWO MASSIVE ANCHOR CORDS carry heavy L2 trees: C2 W val=226 with 5 L2 children (L2 sum=73, 32 percent of parent) + C27 W val=209 with 3 L2 children including pika interrogative. Together 226+209=435 = 41 percent of grand total = dominant entries (population totals, areas, or tribute). Pure cadastral color scheme: geography AB + measurements W + labels MB + owners BG. Musee Quai Branly, Paris (cat. 71.1878.3.61). Incomplete.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  88 cords (L1: 49, L2: 39). Architecture: 0-SEUL with heavy L2 trees (L2/L1 = 0.80).
  6 colors: AB(39, 44%), W(21, 24%), MB(19, 22%), BG(7, 8%), KB(1), GG(1).
  6 clusters. INCOMPLETE.
  Provenance: UNKNOWN. Museum: Musee du Quai Branly, Paris (cat. 71.1878.3.61).
  Date: 23 NOVEMBER 1487 CE (HIGH confidence).
  5 STRING (100 percent dict hit), 78 INT, 5 EMPTY (10 percent).

DATING -- 23 NOVEMBER 1487 CE
  Mode C (displaced year):
    C1=12  -- FLAG (indicates month 12 or category code)
    C3=49  -- YEAR OFFSET (1438+49=1487 CE)
    C5=11  -- MONTH (November)
    C6=23  -- DAY (23)
  Validated by independent ALBA session. Within the 1458-1536 range.
  The full day/month/year dating convention is unusual -- 74 percent of dated khipus carry
  year + month only.

KEY VOCABULARY (5 STRING, 100 percent dict hit)
  maqa x3 -- BOUNDARY MARKER (crosses 3 colors MB->AB->AB/W) -- KEY ACTOR
  pata x1 -- TERRACE / PLOT (MB) -- CLOSING WORD confirms register = LAND
  pika x1 -- WHO-PASSIVE (MB L2 on C27) = 'who was assigned/affected?' -- RESPONSIBILITY QUERY

COLOR-AS-ROLE
  AB (44%): GEOGRAPHIC / SUBJECT -- terrain data, measurements.
  W  (24%): PRIMARY DATA -- carries LARGEST VALUES (226, 209).
  MB (22%): CATEGORY -- ALL 3 L1 STRING words on MB.
  BG (8%):  IDENTITY -- owner/responsible-party markers.
  KB (1):   minor.
  GG (1):   minor (possible juridical single-point marker).
  Pure cadastral color scheme: geography(AB) + measurements(W) + labels(MB) + owners(BG).

STRUCTURE -- TWO ANCHOR CORDS
  C2 (W, val=226, 5 L2 children): L2 sum=73 (32 percent of parent). PRIMARY PARCEL.
  C27 (W, val=209, 3 L2 children including pika): SECOND MAJOR PARCEL with RESPONSIBILITY QUERY.
  Together 226 + 209 = 435 = 41 PERCENT OF GRAND TOTAL. Dominant entries = total populations,
  areas, or tribute.
  BG (identity) cords at three key positions: within main measurement (C5), near interrogative (C30),
  and closing (C48) = the PEOPLE associated with the parcels.
narrative:
Twenty-three November, fourteen eighty-seven. Fourteen years before UR176's Chuquitanta murder case was recorded. Thirty years after the Arica observatory pair AS069 and AS070 opened their log. A full day-month-year date carried on cords one, three, five, and six of a land register that the scribe tied at one of the great Inca provincial centres in the last decades before the Spanish arrived.

The first monumental entry is at cord two. Two hundred and twenty-six units on white thread -- the largest single value in the document. Below it, five subsidiary cords detailing the breakdown. The L2 cords sum to seventy-three, about a third of the parent value. A primary parcel with its internal distribution noted.

At cord twenty-seven, the second monumental entry. Two hundred and nine units, on white again. Below it, three subsidiary cords -- and one of them carries a word: pika. Who-passive. 'Who was assigned to this parcel?' or 'Who was affected by the redistribution?' The interrogative is buried in the tree, attached to a specific measurement, asking the administrative question that the numbers alone cannot answer.

Two hundred and twenty-six plus two hundred and nine makes four hundred and thirty-five. Forty-one percent of the grand total of the register. Two cords, two parcels, nearly half the entire document's value. The primary administrative entities the scribe was asked to measure.

Three times the boundary marker: maqa. On medium-brown first, then light-brown, then white. Crossing three colours means crossing three administrative dimensions -- category, geography, data. The boundaries named once in each frame.

The last substantive word at cord forty-eight: pata. Terrace / plot. The document names its subject explicitly at the close: this is about LAND. And beside it, on beige-green thread -- the identity colour -- an owner marker. The parcel has a name attached.

At the Musee du Quai Branly in Paris, catalogue 71.1878.3.61, UR189 rests alongside UR188 (the wall-focused cadastre) and UR192 (the Huaca Perez tambo accounting). The Quai Branly trio. Three Peruvian khipus acquired together in the nineteenth century, three different administrative functions at three possibly different sites, catalogued consecutively and held on the same shelf in Paris since the late nineteenth century. Incomplete, as the OKR note records -- but what remains carries a precise date, two major parcels, a responsibility query, and the material evidence that on the twenty-third of November 1487 CE an Inca administrator was counting land.

prior_work.claim: Mode C displaced-year dating (epoch 1438, year offset on C3, month on C5, day on C6) is documented across 64 khipus in the analysed corpus; the full day/month/year convention of UR189 (month 11, day 23) is rarer than the year-plus-month convention.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive -- Modes A/B/C reference framework.

prior_work.claim: The Musee du Quai Branly in Paris holds UR188 + UR189 + UR192 (the Quai Branly trio) under consecutive catalogue numbering from 1878/1887 acquisitions.
prior_work.citation: Musee du Quai Branly, Paris catalog; UR189 = 71.1878.3.61.

prior_work.claim: pika (who-PASSIVE) is a documented Quechua interrogative form asking about responsibility or patient-role; its use in administrative contexts marks a formal inquiry into who was assigned or affected.
prior_work.citation: Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario; Cerron-Palomino, R. (1987). Linguistica Quechua.

v3_contribution.claim: HIGH-CONFIDENCE DAY/MONTH/YEAR DATE: 23 November 1487 CE via Mode C. Full day+month+year encoding is rarer than year-plus-month; UR189 provides a day-level-precise Inca administrative timestamp.

evidence.test: 4-cord Mode C dating convention (C1 flag + C3 year + C5 month + C6 day)

v3_contribution.claim: TWO-ANCHOR CORD STRUCTURE: C2 val=226 and C27 val=209 together carry 41 percent of the grand total. Structural evidence for two DOMINANT PARCELS in the register -- the administrative skeleton on which the rest is hung.

PIKA-IN-SUBSIDIARY RESPONSIBILITY QUERY: the who-PASSIVE interrogative on MB L2 of C27 attaches a responsibility question to the second major parcel. First corpus instance of an interrogative-in-subsidiary-tree asking about assigned responsibility on a cadastral document.

evidence.test: pika at L2 subsidiary of anchor cord

v3_contribution.claim: MAQA CROSS-COLOR: boundary marker maqa appears 3 times on 3 different colors (MB, AB, W). The same concept is registered in category, geographic, and data dimensions.

evidence.test: maqa cross-color distribution on cadastral document

v3_contribution.claim: QUAI BRANLY TRIO COMPLETE: UR188 + UR189 + UR192 at the same Paris museum. UR188 = wall-focused cadastre, UR189 = parcel register with pika responsibility, UR192 = Huaca Perez tambo accounting. Three different administrative functions at one institution.

companions.note: QUAI BRANLY TRIO MEMBER -- wall-focused cadastre (kaqa x3 corpus record). UR188 + UR189 at same museum; different cadastral sub-types (wall-enclosure vs parcel-measurement).
QUAI BRANLY TRIO MEMBER -- Huaca Perez / Tambo Inca administrative accounting (70 cords, 0 STRING). Same Paris museum.
Pachacamac cadastral site plan (siqa x5 pyramids with ramps). UR189 is the compact parcel-register variant; UR1095 is the full site-plan variant.
Pachacamac admin cadastre (Sept 1461 CE, kata-thesis). UR189's 1487 date is 26 years after UR1087's 1461 -- two precisely-dated Inca cadastral documents spanning the Wayna Qhapaq reign.
Leymebamba 7-sector cadastral survey (442 cords, full 6/6 signature). UR189 is the compact 88-cord variant with 2-anchor structure.
Pachacamac cadastral VERIFICATION register (chiqa x6 corpus record). UR189 measures; UR196 verifies. Different administrative phases.
Chuquitanta murder dossier (Feb 1519 CE). UR189 (Nov 1487) + UR176 (Feb 1519) = 32-year span of Inca administrative documents with full day/month/year dating.

word_reading.note: 5 STRING at 100 percent dict hit. maqa, pata, pika all dictionary-stable and standard cadastral vocabulary. Small sample size (5 cords) is compensated by the date-cord structural anchor.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey parcel-register sub-type robustly supported by (a) pata closing word, (b) maqa boundary markers, (c) two anchor-cord structure, (d) pika responsibility query, (e) pure cadastral color scheme, (f) precise Mode C date. Sub-type: parcel-register with responsibility inquiry.

narrative.note: The '23 November 1487 two-parcel land register' reading is internally coherent and precisely dated. The pika responsibility inquiry reading is plausible given Gonzalez Holguin 1608 + Cerron-Palomino 1987 grammatical attestation. Quai Branly trio context provides external anchor.

data_integrity.note: 100 percent dict hit on STRING. OKR notes incomplete but 10 percent empty cords not excessive. Mode C dating independently validated. No Ascher layer summarised. L2 subsidiary trees verified on 2 anchor cords.

sections.title: Mode C Date Header
sections.reading: Full day/month/year Mode C date: C1=12 flag + C3=49 year-offset (1487 CE) + C5=11 month (November) + C6=23 day. First documented corpus instance of day-level precision on a Mode C cadastral.

sections.title: First Anchor -- 226-unit Parcel
sections.reading: C2 W val=226 with 5 L2 children (L2 sum=73, 32 percent of parent). The PRIMARY PARCEL of the register. Measurement + breakdown + identity markers on BG cords nearby.

key_cords.note: OWNER / RESPONSIBLE PARTY in parcel zone

sections.title: Second Anchor -- 209-unit Parcel + Responsibility Query
sections.reading: C27 W val=209 with 3 L2 children INCLUDING MB pika interrogative = 'who was assigned / affected?' Second major parcel with responsibility query in its subsidiary tree. BG identity cord at C30 nearby.

key_cords.note: RESPONSIBILITY QUERY 'who was assigned?' in subsidiary

sections.title: Boundary Markers + Closing Pata
sections.reading: maqa x3 cross-color boundary markers (MB, AB, W) distributed through the register. C48 MB pata = 'terrace / plot' = CLOSING WORD confirming the register is about LAND. BG identity cord at C48 attaches an owner to the plot.

key_cords.note: LAST WORD = TERRACE / PLOT (register is about LAND)

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive -- Modes A/B/C reference framework (UR189 = 23 Nov 1487 Mode C).
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- pika interrogative form.
Musee du Quai Branly, Paris -- UR189 catalogue 71.1878.3.61.

Compare UR188 (Quai Branly trio wall-cadastre), UR192 (Quai Branly trio tambo accounting), UR1095 (Pachacamac site plan), UR1087 (Pachacamac admin cadastre Sept 1461), UR016 (Leymebamba 7-sector), UR196 (Pachacamac verification register), UR176 (Chuquitanta 1519 murder dossier).
